D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Banknote OVI: Tilt to Shift

the colour-shifting denomination on a banknote is thin-film interference — tilt the note and the reflected peak slides toward blue

Optically variable ink (OVI) is not a pigment but an interference coating. Light reflecting off the top and bottom of a thin film travels different distances; wavelengths that reinforce dominate the colour. The extra path is 2·n·d·cos θ, so as you tilt the note the reinforced wavelength shortens — the famous green-to-blue flip that a colour photocopier cannot fake.

Tilt the note and watch the security patch shift colour as the reflectance peak (below) slides toward shorter wavelengths. Change the film thickness to retune which two colours the note flips between.
